text:
Aussie football fans have been rocked by mixed emotions after the Matildas' gold medal dreams were dashed following a devastating loss to the United States.

Aussie football fans have been devastated and outraged after the Matildas suffered a crushing loss in its match up with the United States of America in the 2024 Paris Olympic Games.

The Australian women’s team only needed a draw to make it out of the group stage and secure a spot in the knockout round, but the final 2-1 result on Thursday morning (AEST) dashed their hopes of progressing.

Following the loss, the Matildas needed Canada to either lose or draw in its match with Colombia to keep gold dreams alive but a 1-0 victory for the Canadians killed the Aussies’ Olympics run.

A win for the Matildas in the USA match looked unlikely, with the side on the back foot against the opposition's fierce attack.

Leading 2-0 at 90 minutes, the Yanks seemed to have it in the bag until defender-turned-striker Alanna Kennedy slotted one in the back of the neck.

A follow-up goal would unfortunately allude the new striker just minutes later when a header opportunity was not secured.

The Matildas' X account shared a heartfelt message with its fans following its Paris departure that was overwhelmingly responded with messages of support.

“While this four-year journey has ended, another one is just around the corner. We ask that you stay with us, the Matildas family, as our story continues to unfold,” it wrote.

Devastated fans shared their heartbreak in response to the post with many saying how proud they were of the team.

Thank You, Australia ðŸ«¶ðŸ’šðŸ’›Thank you for being with us and lifting us throughout every part of our journey over the past four years. For the late nights, the early wake-up calls, the joys and disappointments, and most importantly, for your unwavering supportâ€”win, lose, or draw.â€¦pic.twitter.com/4dWaGNST0o

“If any Australian girls need a big hug or some arms to cry into, to be consoled after this I’m here,” one fan wrote.

Another shared: “Disappointed but proud.”

Others, however, lashed out at the team’s management, particularly its coach Tony Gustavsson who is rumoured to finish up in his position after the Paris Games.

“It’s a fail unfortunately. Poor coaching & tactics. Likely a few retirements in coming weeks to allow the next gen to progress,” wrote one.

Another said: “#TonyOut.”

One quipped: “Let this be a lesson. Never again a foreign coach!"

The game itself was not without controversy as a questionable decision from referee Francois Letexier rocked many fans who were left with seeing red.

Just two minutes before half time the Americans put their first goal in the back of the net, but an impassioned request from Gustavsson, that saw him earn a yellow card, halted a final decision from the referee.

US forward Trinity Rodman scored off a corner but Australia’s goalkeeper had her line of sight disrupted by an offside player, spurring the Aussies to contest the goal.

After a technological mishap that contributed to a 10-minute delay on a final decision, Letexier gave America the goal.

One enraged fan lashed out at the controversial call, while others claimed the Aussies had no hope regardless.

“The girls were robbed,” one player posted.

A user responded to the claim: “Did you watch the game? They hardly had any control of the field or the ball. It’s remarkable that they still scored, more remarkable that they kept it a low scoring game.”

Another replied: “They’ve been poor all tournament. Hardly robbed”

The quarter finals follow the end of the group stage with the USA facing off against Japan, Canada versing Germany, Spain heading toe-to-toe with Colombia and host-nation France battling Brazil.
